title: Brachypodium retusum
intro: Brachypodium retusum, also known as False Oat-grass, is a perennial plant species in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Brachypodium retusum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Brachypodium retusum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a tincture or syrup to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Brachypodium retusum is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Brachypodium, which includes several species of grasses. The genus Brachypodium is part of the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. This classification places Brachypodium retusum within the monocotyledonous flowering plants. Further classification also includes it being part of the kingdom Plantae.
